cap_non_applicability_reasons.max_finite_difference_evaluations: IE4 does not use finite-difference probes.
cap_non_applicability_reasons.max_replications: IE4 is deterministic and records replication_count=1.
cap_non_applicability_reasons.max_sinkhorn_iterations: IE4 does not instantiate Sinkhorn transport.
cap_non_applicability_reasons.max_time_steps: IE4 evaluates single-step affine parity only and does not roll out a time-series filter.

explanatory_only_diagnostics: Closed-form affine algebra parity does not support nonlinear-flow integration claims.
Closed-form affine algebra parity does not support filtering or posterior-quality claims.

mcse_or_interval.summary: Not applicable because IE4 uses deterministic affine fixtures with replication_count=1.

non_implication: IE4 PF-PF algebra parity checks validate only closed-form proposal-density, Jacobian-sign, unnormalized corrected-log-weight, and normalized-weight parity on deterministic affine clean-room fixtures. They do not validate nonlinear flow integration, solver stability, filtering correctness, production bayesfilter code, real DPF-HMC targets, posterior quality, banking use, model-risk use, or production readiness.
post_run_red_team_note: These affine checks could still pass while nonlinear flow integration, solver stability, and downstream filtering logic remain wrong because the fixture never leaves closed-form affine algebra.
